questions.question: What is the distributional hypothesis?
options: You shall know a word by the company it keeps
Words are uniformly distributed across documents
All words can be embedded in 300 dimensions
Frequent words carry the most meaning
questions.explanation: Firth's (1957) distributional hypothesis: similar contexts imply similar meanings.
options_zh: 你能从一个词的搭配中认识它
所有词都可以嵌入到 300 维
questions.explanation_zh: Firth (1957) 的分布假说：相似上下文意味着相似含义。

questions.question: Why is plain softmax over the vocabulary impractical in Word2Vec?
options: Computing softmax over 100k+ vocabulary terms is prohibitively expensive per training step
Softmax cannot model probabilities
Softmax has no gradient
It causes vanishing gradients
questions.explanation: Full-vocabulary softmax is too expensive; negative sampling reformulates it as binary classification.
questions.question_zh: 为什么在 Word2Vec 中对整个词表跑普通 softmax 不实用？
options_zh: 对 100k+ 词表的 softmax 在每步训练中代价过高
questions.explanation_zh: 全词表 softmax 太昂贵；负采样把它重新表述为二分类。

questions.question: What is the difference between skip-gram and CBOW?
options: Skip-gram uses bigrams; CBOW uses unigrams
Skip-gram predicts context words from the center; CBOW predicts the center from context
Skip-gram predicts the center word from context; CBOW does the reverse
Skip-gram trains a deep network; CBOW is shallow
questions.explanation: Skip-gram: center -> context. CBOW: context -> center.
questions.question_zh: skip-gram 和 CBOW 的区别是什么？
options_zh: skip-gram 用 bigram；CBOW 用 unigram
skip-gram 从中心词预测上下文词；CBOW 从上下文预测中心词
skip-gram 从上下文预测中心词；CBOW 反过来
skip-gram 训深网络；CBOW 是浅层的

questions.question: In negative sampling, what is the objective for the positive pair (center, context)?
options: Maximize sigmoid(W[center] dot W'[context]) so it is close to 1
Force them to be orthogonal
Minimize their dot product
Decorrelate the vectors
questions.explanation: Positive pairs train sigmoid near 1; sampled negatives train sigmoid near 0.
questions.question_zh: 在负采样中，正样本对 (center, context) 的目标是什么？
options_zh: 最大化 sigmoid(W[center] dot W'[context])，使其接近 1
questions.explanation_zh: 正样本对训练 sigmoid 接近 1；采样的负样本训练 sigmoid 接近 0。

questions.question: After training Word2Vec, which weight matrix becomes the word embeddings?
options: A separate post-training projection
The input-to-hidden W matrix (center-word table)
Both, multiplied together
The hidden-to-output W' matrix
questions.explanation: The center-word table W is the standard embedding output; W' is often discarded or averaged in.
questions.question_zh: 训练完 Word2Vec 后，哪个权重矩阵成为词 embedding？
options_zh: input 到 hidden 的 W 矩阵（中心词表）
hidden 到 output 的 W' 矩阵
questions.explanation_zh: 中心词表 W 是标准的 embedding 输出；W' 通常被丢弃或被平均合并。

questions.question: Why does Word2Vec fail on polysemy (e.g. 'bank')?
options: The window size is too small
It ignores rare words
Negative sampling drops rare meanings
It assigns one static vector per word, so 'river bank' and 'financial bank' share the same vector
questions.explanation: Static embeddings cannot disambiguate senses; contextual embeddings (ELMo/BERT) fix this.
questions.question_zh: 为什么 Word2Vec 在多义词（例如 'bank'）上失败？
options_zh: 它对每个词分配一个静态向量，所以 'river bank' 和 'financial bank' 共享同一向量
questions.explanation_zh: 静态 embedding 无法消歧词义；上下文 embedding（ELMo/BERT）解决了这个问题。

questions.question: When would you still reach for Word2Vec over a transformer in 2026?
options: Lightweight, on-device, domain-specific retrieval where a single row lookup is the latency budget
When you need contextual disambiguation
When inputs are very long
When dialog quality matters
questions.explanation: Word2Vec wins on tiny latency budgets, on-device inference, or fast domain-specific training.
questions.question_zh: 2026 年什么时候你还会选 Word2Vec 而不是 transformer？
options_zh: 轻量、设备端、领域特定的检索，单行查表就是延迟预算
questions.explanation_zh: Word2Vec 在极小延迟预算、设备端推理或快速领域训练时仍占优。

questions.question: What enables the famous analogy 'king - man + woman ~ queen'?
options: The model encodes royalty as a flag bit
Skip-gram trains on royal vocabularies
Vector arithmetic captures linear directions like 'royal' that transfer across genders
Cosine similarity is invariant to gender
questions.explanation: Directions in embedding space encode relational features, so adding/subtracting moves systematically.
questions.question_zh: 是什么让著名的 'king - man + woman ~ queen' 类比成立？
options_zh: 模型把 royalty 编为一个 flag bit
skip-gram 在皇室词表上训练
向量算术捕捉到了像 'royal' 这种可在性别间迁移的线性方向
questions.explanation_zh: embedding 空间中的方向编码了关系特征，所以加减能产生系统性移动。
